content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

You are allowed to perform the following operation on it any number of times:


Choose an index iii such that 1<i<N1 \lt i \lt N1<i<N, and any integer XXX (XXX can be negative).
Then, modify the array as follows:

Subtract XXX from Ai−1A_{i-1}Ai−1​ (that is, Ai−1→Ai−1−XA_{i-1} \to A_{i-1} - XAi−1​→Ai−1​−X)
Add 2X2X2X to AiA_iAi​ (that is, Ai→Ai+2XA_i \to A_i + 2XAi​→Ai​+2X)
Subtract XXX from Ai+1A_{i+1}Ai+1​ (that is, Ai+1→Ai+1−XA_{i+1} \to A_{i+1} - XAi+1​→Ai+1​−X)


Your task is to find the minimum number of operations required to convert the array AAA into a palindrome†^\dagger†.

If it is not possible to do so, output −1-1−1 instead.

†^\dagger† An integer array AAA of length NNN is called a palindrome iff Ai=AN+1−iA_i = A_{N+1-i}Ai​=AN+1−i​ for every i=1,2,3,…,Ni = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, Ni=1,2,3,…,N.

For example, [−3,35,35,−3][-3, 35, 35, -3][−3,35,35,−3] is a palindrome, while [12,21][12, 21][12,21] is not.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two line of input.

The first line contains a single integer NNN, the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of operations required to make the array AAA a palindrome.

If it is not possible to do so, output −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4⋅1054 \cdot 10^54⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can't convert the array AAA into a palindrome.
Test case 222: We do the following operation:

Choose i=2i = 2i=2, X=−3X = -3X=−3. The array AAA becomes [3,0,0,3][3, 0, 0, 3][3,0,0,3].
This is a palindrome.

Test case 333: The array AAA is already a palindrome, so no operations are needed.
